---
title: "About"
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/johngolden
---

This is a collection of pedagogical write-ups on quantum computing — short,
self-contained notes that work through the math and intuition behind ideas
I want to understand more deeply.

Each post pairs prose and equations with figures generated by standalone
Python scripts in the [`code/`](https://github.com/johngolden/website/tree/main/code)
directory. The scripts are production-style files meant to be downloaded and
reused, not notebook cells. Each script declares its own dependencies
inline ([PEP 723](https://peps.python.org/pep-0723/)) and runs with a
single `uv run code/<script>.py` — no virtualenv to manage. Running
`make figures` from the repo root regenerates every plot, so the figures
in posts always match the downloadable code.

## Conventions

- Math uses standard LaTeX (`$...$` inline, `$$...$$` display).
- Display equations are numbered when referenced; cross-references use
  Quarto's `@eq-...`, `@fig-...`, `@sec-...` syntax.
- References are kept in a single BibTeX file and cited inline.
- Code shown in prose is **inert** — syntax-highlighted snippets only.
  Nothing executes at build time.

## Reusing the code

Every figure is produced by a script in `code/`. Grab the file directly
from GitHub (or the `code.zip` bundle on the site) and run it with
[`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/):

```sh
uv run code/bloch_sphere.py
```

The script's dependencies are declared inline, so `uv` resolves and
caches everything it needs without polluting your environment.
